reasoning: This reduction kernel performs 1 FLOP (addition) per 4 bytes loaded, yielding an arithmetic intensity of ~0.25 FLOP/byte, which is far below the A10G ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. The kernel streams through 256 MB of input data with minimal computation, making it squarely memory-bandwidth limited. With 4096 blocks × 256 threads = 1M threads performing a grid-stride loop over 64M elements, DRAM utilization will be high as the workload is sufficient to saturate the memory subsystem.
